summary: Fiery set with standout Brown Eyed Women, China Doll and Loose Lucy
blurb: The first half delivers tight, well‑paced songs while the second half stays punchy without over‑relying on jam vehicles. Highlights include an incendiary Brown Eyed Women, a gorgeous China Doll, and a swinging Loose Lucy with Jerry’s vocals on fire throughout.
review:
September 18, 1993 at Madison Square Garden proved to be one of the Dead’s most electrifying charity gigs. The band opened with a crisp Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo that let Jerry’s voice shine “ON” from the first line, then moved through Little Red Rooster and Althea before hitting a powerhouse Brown Eyed Women that reviewers called “on fire.”  The set stayed tight—Cassidy and Loose Lucy kept the momentum rolling, and the second set traded the usual jam vehicles for a leaner, high‑energy flow: Iko Iko, Victim Or The Crime into Uncle John’s Band > Corinna, followed by a kickass Drums/Space segment that propelled The Wheel and an inspired All Along The Watchtower into a gorgeous China Doll.  The encore’s Gloria capped the night with pure crowd joy.
All three recordings—soundboard, audience, and a decent unknown source—agree the show was “fiery” and “vibrant,” praising Jerry’s searing solos, his vocal fire, and the drums’ extra‑kick energy.  With crystal‑clear instrument separation on the Miller SBD, this performance stands out as a must‑listen snapshot of the Dead’s late‑career spark.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are positive, repeatedly praising Jerry’s fire‑brand vocals and solos, the kickass drums/space segment, and standout songs like Brown Eyed Women, China Doll and Loose Lucy. Consistent high praise across sources warrants a strong sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture with excellent instrument separation and no dropouts, preserving the full energy of the performance.

key_highlights: Jerry’s vocal performance is described as “ON” and his guitar work as “on fire.”
The drums/space segment is highlighted as kickass, adding a burst of energy.
China Doll receives special praise for its gorgeous phrasing, rounding out a tight set.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia sings and solos with blazing intensity; his voice is described as “on” from the first line and his guitar work called ‘on fire’ throughout.
band_performance.Mickey: The drums/space segment is highlighted as kickass, adding extra energy to the second set.
